portfolio: Base Enterprise Agent & Asset Operations Portfolio

claims.claim: The Base portfolio covers M01-M40 plus ERPNext Projects through fourteen explicit module domains.
claims.boundary: Coverage is product architecture, not implementation or production use in every module.

claims.claim: BaseProofPay is a Base-native Treasury/Payment component with verified contracts, x402 technical use, ERC-8021 attribution and draft-only ERP controls.
claims.boundary: BaseProofPay is not the portfolio umbrella; ACC-PAY is synthetic and independent adoption remains missing.

claims.claim: BaseBatchTwin and BaseInventoryRoot have a local CATVERSE schema, state machine, fixture suite and fail-closed SLE mapping.
claims.boundary: No token or root is deployed, no ERP document is created, and synthetic SLE is not production inventory.

claims.claim: Base owns CATVERSE inventory quantity, valuation or legal title.
claims.boundary: ERP Stock Ledger and physical controls remain authoritative; Base evidence twins are non-title references.

claims.claim: Manufacturing/Quality evidence and cost-root interaction are designed for BOM, WIP, yield, scrap, quality, variance and repost states.
claims.boundary: No production fixture or cost root is implemented; Base never calculates final cost.

claims.claim: BaseAssetEvidence, BaseFinancingIntent and BaseCloseProof have explicit lifecycle and human-control boundaries.
claims.boundary: No asset title, collateral, financing approval, period close or report root is claimed.

claims.claim: The portfolio has one genuinely independent voluntary payer.
claims.boundary: Self-controlled canaries, Talent aggregates and synthetic ERP documents cannot satisfy adoption.

claims.claim: The portfolio performs posted accounting, reconciled close or actual FP&A reporting.
claims.boundary: Current ERP evidence stops at owner-only drafts; actual, draft and forecast must remain separate.

claims.claim: All module interactions preserve least privilege and chain-data minimization.
claims.boundary: Each future DocType, deployment and public action still needs its own acceptance gate.

claims.claim: Every Base component is designed to feed one AOXPET/USD 2026-08 synthetic closed-period account set through a C0-C7 trace path.
claims.boundary: This is the final acceptance target, not evidence that the account set or period is closed.

claims.claim: The AOXPET CATVERSE 2026-08 period has completed C0-C7, human posting, reconciliation, close and report delivery.
claims.boundary: Current evidence is architecture, payment draft-only controls and a local inventory model; the closed period is not complete.

claims.claim: BaseBatchTwin, InventoryRoot, AssetEvidence, FinancingIntent and CloseProof are complete only when crosswalked to ERP source documents, vouchers, ledgers, close and reports.
claims.boundary: A schema, fixture, token, hash or state root alone cannot complete a module slice.

claims.claim: Payment and wallet expansion is frozen as maintenance; BaseProofPay is only one Treasury/Payment component.
claims.boundary: Existing payment P0 remains, but new payment/finality work cannot be counted as product progress.

claims.claim: The Base edition produces a deterministic inventory evidence bundle without a new transaction or ERP write.
claims.boundary: The bundle proves local reproducibility and controls, not deployment, production inventory or closed-period completion.

claims.claim: ERP inventory linkage was tested with GET-only probes and returned an exact reproducible blocker packet.
claims.boundary: Item is readable; six inventory DocTypes deny read permission. No SLE valuation, repost, reconciliation or report readback is claimed.

claims.claim: R3 closed-period evidence is complete.
claims.boundary: Posting, reconciliation, close and report evidence remain missing.

claims.claim: R4 submission package is ready for external use.
claims.boundary: The local package must still pass final QA and be published from the same release fingerprint before any external application or nomination.

claims.claim: The Base Dashboard app registration exists for this builder surface.
claims.boundary: The new Enterprise OS deployment URL is not registered until a separately confirmed Dashboard update is completed.

claims.claim: Builder attribution is configured with Builder Code bc_iscm570t and ERC-8021-compatible evidence.
claims.boundary: Configuration and onchain attribution evidence do not guarantee immediate Base.dev indexing, ranking or rewards.

claims.claim: Talent indexes the BaseProofPay project and currently displays aggregate activity evidence.
claims.boundary: The aggregate has no transaction hashes and cannot prove an independent payer or product adoption.

claims.claim: Base.dev has fully refreshed and ranked the current Enterprise OS release.
claims.boundary: Indexing delay must be handled by read-only refresh and support evidence, never by repeating transactions.

claims.claim: The portfolio is already accepted into a Base Builders list or program.
claims.boundary: Only a local nomination packet exists. Publication, nomination and acceptance are separate externally confirmed states.

publication_rule: Only supported claims may be used with adjacent boundaries. design_only, missing_p0 and prohibited claims block implementation, adoption and production language.
